requirements: ***Please Note: This is a fully remote role; however, international travel is expected to collaborate with global R&D labs, business units, and pilot customers, and may reach 30 to 50% depending on project needs and incubation phase.*** About Lenovo CTO Org Lenovo CTO Org is the cutting-edge R&D and technology incubation organization of Lenovo, dedicated to advancing information technology, smart devices, and services. Our mission is to turn breakthrough innovations into real businesses, powering Lenovo's most advanced products and services and shaping the future of AI, hybrid computing, edge technologies, and beyond. About the Role This is a leadership role at the heart of Lenovo CTO Org's Tech Incubation team, accountable for the end-to-end delivery of solutions across a portfolio of concurrent incubation projects. You will build, lead, and scale a multidisciplinary delivery organization as the portfolio evolves. You will establish and scale a new Solution Delivery organization, creating the processes, operating model, and team required to support a growing incubation portfolio. This is not a single-program management role. You will operate as the central orchestrator across multiple incubation projects — dynamically prioritizing resources, resolving cross-functional bottlenecks, and ensuring alignment between engineering, product, business development, and customer stakeholders. Success requires the ability to context-switch across projects, reallocate talent based on shifting priorities, and maintain delivery momentum in a dynamic environment. Our incubation portfolio spans AI-driven products across smart manufacturing, full-stack AI agent solutions, robotics, sports technology, and other emerging domains. Each project moves at speed through the 0→1 journey — from concept validation and pilot deployment to repeatable commercial engagements. Success is measured not only by delivering successful pilots, but by helping transform incubation projects into repeatable, scalable commercial solutions ready for transition into Lenovo business units.Your role is to ensure every solution is delivered with excellence, on time, and with the quality and scalability required to transition successfully to Lenovo's business units. You bring a customer-first, results-driven mindset to everything you do. This is a US-based role operating within a global environment; international travel is expected to collaborate with R&D labs, pilot customers, and business units, and may range from approximately 30–50% depending on project needs and incubation phase. Prioritize across a portfolio of projects rather than managing only one fixed program. Continuously assess project priority, risk, and strategic value to allocate delivery resources where they matter most. Define and enforce delivery standards, methodologies, and quality gates appropriate for early-stage, fast-moving environments without introducing unnecessary overhead. Establish delivery standards, deployment playbooks, and operating processes for the newly formed Solution Delivery organization. Establish clear delivery metrics and dashboards that provide real-time visibility into project health, risks, and resource utilization across the portfolio. Serve as the ultimate escalation point for delivery blockers, cross-functional conflicts, and critical customer-facing issues. Cross-Project Orchestration & Resource Optimization Dynamically prioritize and allocate delivery resources across incubation projects based on strategic priority, customer commitments, and business milestones. Manage interdependencies between concurrent projects, ensuring knowledge, reusable components, and best practices are shared effectively. Partner with Product Managers to align delivery scope with roadmap timelines and incubation stage gates. Decompose complex delivery objectives into actionable workstreams; coordinate resources across teams and geographies to drive execution toward goals. Customer & Stakeholder Alignment Own customer onboarding, adoption, satisfaction, and long-term success for deployed incubation solutions, ensuring pilot engagements deliver measurable business value and create pathways for expansion. Engage directly with strategic pilot customers and solution partners on delivery matters, ensuring commitments are met and customer satisfaction remains high. Act as the primary delivery interface to senior internal stakeholders, including CTO leadership, business unit heads, and R&D lab directors. Ensure seamless handover of successfully incubated solutions to business unit engineering and operations teams, including documentation, training, and transition planning. Help turn incubation projects into repeatable commercial solutions by capturing delivery insights and feeding them back to the solution R&D team. Champion operational best practices — including risk management, change control, configuration management, and field support processes — adapted for incubation speed. You will drive revenue growth by identifying new business opportunities, cultivating relationships with key decision-makers, and delivering tailored solutions that meet client needs. Build and maintain strong relationships with key decision-makers at target accounts. Understand and effectively communicate our service offerings to executives, ensuring alignment with client needs. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design and deliver customized solutions. Maintain a strong sales pipeline and consistently meet or exceed revenue targets. Executive Director, Global ESG & Regulatory Compliance Reporting to: SVP, Chief Legal & Corporate Responsibility Officer Why Work at Lenovo We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers. Lenovo is a US$83 billion global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers in 180 markets. Our continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. Description and Requirements Lenovo is seeking a strategic, business-solutions-driven leader to serve as Executive Director, Global ESG & Regulatory Compliance. This role reports to the SVP, Chief Legal & Corporate Responsibility Officer and is responsible for leading Lenovo’s global ESG reporting, sustainability regulatory compliance, and product environmental compliance programs. This role plays a critical part in enabling business growth and market access by ensuring compliance with evolving global regulatory requirements while supporting customer and investor expectations. In addition, the Executive Director oversees enterprise ISO management systems (ISO 9001, 14001, 50001) and aligns ESG and compliance programs with customer sustainability requirements, ecolabel expectations, and commercial needs. Key responsibilities include leading global ESG disclosures (including HKEX and CSRD readiness), defining enterprise ESG strategy and commitments (including net-zero and 2030 ESG KPIs), and managing external ESG ratings and surveys (e.g., CDP, EcoVadis, MSCI). The role oversees product and environmental regulatory compliance programs and governance structures, including the Product Compliance Review Board and associated tools and processes, and leads the preparation and delivery of ESG updates and materials to executive leadership and the Board of Directors. The role also owns the sys tems, tools, and digital infrastructure that enable ESG reporting and regulatory compliance, including advancing automation and AI-enabled capabilities. The Executive Director will serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership, engaging regularly with executive teams on ESG performance, regulatory developments, and risk management. The role also supports investor engagement, customer ESG requirements, and external interactions with regulators and industry groups to anticipate and influence evolving stand ards. This position leads a global, multi-disciplinary team spanning ESG reporting, regulatory compliance, and ISO management systems, and is responsible for building scalable processes and capabilities to manage increasing regulatory complexity. requirements: We are looking for a Finance DT Associate Specialist to join the Finance Digital Transformation team. Strong learning agility, logical thinking, hands-on attitude, ownership, and willingness to work in a cross-functional environment. Good communication skills in both Chinese and English. Experience in business analysis, user research, process mapping, requirement documentation, workshop support, dashboard creation, or automation solution design is a plus. requirements: About the Role We are looking for a Firmware Validation Engineer to develop and maintain a comprehensive firmware validation framework for platform firmware components, including BMC, UEFI, and SMM . This role focuses on designing validation strategies, developing automated test solutions, integrating test infrastructure with Jenkins, and driving continuous quality improvements through close collaboration with firmware development teams. Key Responsibilities Review BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware design specifications and architecture documents to develop comprehensive validation plans and test cases. Design, develop, and maintain automated test scripts and validation frameworks for BMC, UEFI, and SMM firmware components. Develop and maintain Jenkins-based test pipelines to support both automated and on-demand firmware validation execution. Integrate validation tools and test scripts developed by different testing teams into a unified Jenkins pipeline infrastructure. Manage, maintain, and upgrade Jenkins environments to ensure the stability, scalability, and reliability of the firmware testing platform. Analyze test results, identify failures, and work closely with firmware developers to drive issue resolution and root-cause analysis. Conduct regular reviews of validation coverage, test effectiveness, and execution efficiency with firmware development teams to continuously improve product quality. Experience in firmware validation, system validation, or software test automation. Proficiency in scripting or programming languages such as Python, Shell Script, PowerShell, or similar. Hands-on experience with Jenkins pipeline development, CI/CD infrastructure, and automation frameworks. Familiarity with Linux and Windows operating environments. Experience reviewing technical specifications and translating requirements into effective test cases. Additional

requirements: Business Development Manager (BDM) com experiência no mercado de tecnologia com o grande objetivo de expandir a presença da Lenovo no mercado de monitores no Brasil. O profissional será responsável por impulsionar o crescimento do negócio, identificar novas oportunidades e fortalecer o relacionamento com parceiros e clientes. Principais Atividades e Responsabilidades: 1. Estratégia de Vendas e Crescimento: Planejar e executar a estratégia de vendas para o portfólio de monitores, visando o aumento da receita e da participação de mercado. Identificar e desenvolver novos canais de vendas e parcerias estratégicas (revendas, varejistas, distribuidores, etc.). Analisar o mercado e a concorrência para identificar tendências, oportunidades e ameaças, e ajustar a estratégia de forma proativa. 2. Gestão de Parceiros e Relacionamento: Cultivar e fortalecer o relacionamento com parceiros-chave (distribuidores e principais parceiros), garantindo o alinhamento de metas e a execução de planos de negócio. Capacitar as equipes de vendas dos parceiros sobre os diferenciais e as novas tecnologias dos monitores Lenovo. Negociar acordos comerciais e programas de incentivo que impulsionem as vendas e a rentabilidade. 3. Apresentações e Interações com Clientes: Conduzir apresentações de produto para clientes diretos (empresas) e indiretos (através dos parceiros), destacando os benefícios e o valor dos monitores Lenovo. Representar a marca em feiras, eventos e reuniões de negócios, atuando como o porta-voz do nosso negócio de monitores. Apoiar a equipe de vendas em negociações complexas, fornecendo expertise técnica e comercial para fechar grandes negócios. 4. Gestão de Portfólio e Produtos: Atuar como o especialista do portfólio de monitores, fornecendo feedback do mercado para as equipes de produto e marketing. Definir o mix de produtos ideal para o mercado local, considerando as necessidades dos clientes e a competitividade dos preços. Lançar e promover novos produtos, garantindo que as equipes de vendas e os parceiros estejam preparados para comercializá-los com sucesso. 5. Análise de Dados e Reporte: Monitorar e analisar o desempenho de vendas (sell-in e sell-out) e os indicadores-chave de performance (KPIs). Elaborar relatórios periódicos para a liderança, apresentando resultados, desafios e planos de ação. Utilizar dados de mercado para tomar decisões estratégicas e otimizar a alocação de recursos. 6. Suporte ao Planejamento de Supply Chain: Colaborar com a equipe de Supply Chain , fornecendo insights sobre a demanda do mercado e as tendências de vendas para otimizar o planejamento de estoque e a disponibilidade de produtos. requirements: Join Lenovo, a global technology leader driving innovation across AI, data center solutions, smart devices, and digital transformation. As the world's #1 provider of supercomputing systems, we help leading organizations solve complex challenges through cutting-edge HPC (High Performance Computing) and AI technologies. We're looking for an HPC System Administrator to join our Data Center Operations team in Munich, Germany. In this customer-facing role, you'll support and optimize advanced HPC and AI environments, working with Linux systems, servers, storage, networking, and data center infrastructure while ensuring secure, reliable, and high-performing platforms for leading enterprises and research organizations. What You Will Do: Act as the primary technical contact (SPOC) for customers, providing expert support across HPC (High Performance Computing) and AI platforms while building strong long-term customer relationships. Monitor and maintain critical data center infrastructure, including servers, storage, networking, operating systems, cluster management software, power, cooling, and environmental systems to ensure reliability, availability, and security. Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, operating system, firmware, network, and infrastructure issues, coordinating with vendors and internal teams to quickly minimize downtime and service disruptions. Manage daily system administration activities, including user access management, software deployment, OS upgrades, firmware updates, security patching, defect resolution, and ongoing platform maintenance. Take ownership of incident and problem management by responding to system alerts, monitoring performance and system health, identifying root causes, opening vendor support tickets, and tracking issues through resolution. Support the installation, configuration, testing, upgrade, and optimization of HPC and AI environments, helping customers continuously improve their research computing capabilities and platform performance. Work closely with customer technology, infrastructure, security, governance, and vendor teams to deliver projects, implement controlled changes, maintain documentation, ensure compliance, and support audits and service reviews. Provide technical guidance, knowledge transfer, and training to customers and researchers, contribute to documentation and best practices, participate in projects and customer meetings, and help maximize the value of HPC resources through outstanding customer support and analytical problem-solving skills. What We Look For: Strong Linux administration knowledge, including SUSE, RHEL, and CentOS environments. Hands-on expertise with Confluent and Slurm in enterprise or HPC environments. Background in system administration, monitoring, troubleshooting, and technical support of complex infrastructure. Ability to independently perform operating system installations, software deployments, firmware updates, and system upgrades. Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to diagnose, investigate, and resolve complex technical issues. Experience supporting mission-critical systems, ensuring reliability, performance, and operational stability. Fluent English communication skills, both written and spoken, with the ability to work directly with international customers. Customer-focused mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of providing high-quality technical support and guidance. This position requires significant hands-on experience with Windows Server platforms, Active Directory, systems management, and security configurations, as well as strong working knowledge of Linux-based systems in production environments. The candidate will be responsible for building, configuring, optimizing, patching, maintaining, and securing both Windows and Linux systems. This includes applying security fixes and remediation plans, documenting procedures, diagramming systems, responding to alerts and incidents through the ticketing system, and maintaining proper configuration and operational records. On the Windows side, the role includes administration and support of technologies such as Active Directory, Microsoft SQL Server, Exchange, and SharePoint. On the Linux side, the role includes administration of enterprise Linux distributions (such as Red Hat, Rocky, or Ubuntu), managing system services, performance tuning, patching, log analysis, storage and filesystem management, and supporting applications hosted on Linux platforms. In addition, the candidate will be required to learn and support applications running on both Windows and Linux systems in order to maintain operational stability. Current applications include SolarWinds Orion, Password Manager Pro, AD Manager, and AD Audit, as well as Linux-hosted services supporting monitoring, automation, or infrastructure tooling. Critical Skills/Qualifications: Advanced Windows Server administration skills with certification and 5+ years of experience, or 10+ years of equivalent relevant work experience. Strong Linux system administration experience, including command-line troubleshooting, service management, patching, and security hardening in production environments. Experience supporting Microsoft SQL (including clustered environments), Exchange email systems, or SharePoint. Solid troubleshooting, documentation, and operational support skills across mixed OS environments. Language Skills – fluent in spoken and written English. Bonus: Experience administering mixed Windows and Linux enterprise environments. Experience with email systems, monitoring platforms, or infrastructure management tools. Experience with security remediation, vulnerability management, and system hardening. Familiarity with automation or scripting (PowerShell, Bash, or similar). Additional

と呼ばれる状況ではありますが、私たちは法人向け事業の更なる拡大のため営業組織を強化します。 ■組織について レノボ・ジャパン コマーシャル事業本部 広域営業事業部 中小企業から大企業まで、幅広いお客様を担当しています。チームワークを重んじ、メンバー同士でノウハウを共有、全員で与えられたミッションをクリアすべく日々業務に取り組んでいます。ハイブリッドワークを取り入れ、柔軟な働き方も可能です。 Manager （上司） : Japan Commercial SMB MM Inside Sales Manager Number of Peers （同僚の人数） : 15 ■職務内容 インサイドセールスとして電話、メール、 Web 面談等を効果的に活用してお客様にハードウェア、ソフトウェア、サービスなどを提案し、成約に結び付けていきます。外勤営業との連携は不可欠ですが、外勤営業のサポート役ではなく、お客様のフロントに立って担当セールスチームのハブとなる重要な仕事です。常に最先端の IT 技術に触れることができるので、営業力だけでなく、 IT の知識も自然と身に付きます。 ・お客様ニーズを分析・理解し、意思決定プロセスを把握することで、お客様にとって魅力的な提案をおこなう ・外勤営業、パートナーセールス、製品別市場開発マネージャー、ビジネスパートナーの担当者など社内外関係者と密に連携し、ビジネスチャンスを成功に導く ・個人の営業目標を達成することにより、会社の目標や目的を達成させる ・セールスパイプラインの的確な状況判断により、営業活動および収益達成を正確に予測する ・見込み客への提案からクロージング、製品・サービス導入後のフォロー、次回購入に向けたお客様との関係構築まで包括的にアカウントマネジメントをおこなう ・社内マネジメント層と連携し、お客様トップマネジメントとのリレーションシップ構築を実現する ■ Key Interaction with: ・外勤営業 ・パートナーセールス（ビジネスパートナーを担当する営業） ・ビジネス・ディベロップメント・マネージャー（製品別市場開発マネージャー） ・製品マネージャー ・カスタマーサポート ・オペレーション ・マーケティング ・ファイナンス ・需給管理 ■キャリアパス ・外勤営業 ・インサイドセールスマネージャー ・マーケティング ・製品担当 など 私たちの期待する候補者は、日々アップデートされる専門知識を身につけ、お客様の成功に貢献する情熱をもったプロフェッショナルです。このポジションには業績に応じた競争力のある報酬パッケージ、包括的な福利厚生、専門的な成長と開発の機会を提供します。私たちと一緒にビジネス拡大の旅に出ませんか。興味がある方は、履歴書とカバーレターを人事チームに提出してください。あなたを歓迎できる日を楽しみにしています。 必須となるご経験 3 年以上の PC または IT 業界での営業経験 社会人経験 10 年くらいまで 求められるスキル等 1. コミュニケーションスキル : 営業担当者は製品情報を効果的に伝え、潜在的なお客様を説得する必要があります。電話がメインとなるので優れた口頭および書面によるコミュニケーションスキルが重要です。 2. 交渉スキル : 相互に有益な合意に達するためにお客様・ビジネスパートナーと交渉することがよくあります。高い交渉力は必須のスキルです。 3. 関係構築スキル : お客様との関係を構築し維持することは、インサイドセールスとして不可欠です。営業担当者は、お客様との信頼関係を築き、良好な関係を維持して、リピート ビジネスを促進する必要があります。新しい見込み客を継続的に探し、潜在的なお客様をフォローアップし、成約につなげる意欲が求められます。 4. 製品知識 : 営業担当者は、機能、利点、競争上の優位性など、製品やサービスについて深く理解する必要があります。この知識により、お客様の期待を超える提案が可能となります。 5. 適応性 : 営業担当者は順応性があり、さまざまなお客様や状況に応じてアプローチをすばやく調整できる必要があります。 6. リスニング スキル : 効果的なリスニングスキルは、お客様のニーズを理解し、機会を特定し、懸念に対処するために重要です。営業担当者は、お客様からのフィードバックに積極的に耳を傾け、適切に対応できる必要があります。 7. チームワーク : 営業担当者はお客様に最新・最適な製品サービスを提供するため、チームの一員として活動し他の部門と協力します。チームの目標を達成することがゴールです。 8. 時間管理スキル : お客様の期待に応えるためには、スピーディーな対応・活動が必須です。効果的かつ効率的に時間の優先順位を付けることが重要です。 9. 自発的 : チームの成功のため、自発的・積極的なアクションが求められます。業務範囲外の活動への積極参加は称賛の対象となります。 Additional

requirements: 岗位职责： 1. 项目挖掘与筛选 ：围绕公司投资战略，重点聚焦半导体、人工智能、机器人/具身智能、先进制造等硬科技领域，主动开拓项目渠道，筛选优质早期及成长期投资标的，建立并维护项目来源网络。 2. 尽职调查与投资分析 ：主导或参与项目的业务、财务、法律及技术尽调，组织内外部资源完成深度调研；独立撰写投资建议书、立项报告、投资决策报告等核心文件，对项目进行估值分析和风险判断。 3. 交易执行与谈判 ：参与投资交易结构设计、关键条款谈判及协议签署，协调各方推动项目顺利完成交割。 4. 投后管理与赋能 ：负责对被投企业的日常投后管理工作，跟踪企业经营动态，定期撰写投后报告；依托联想集团产业生态，为被投企业提供技术、市场、供应链等增值赋能支持。 5. 行业研究与战略支持 ：持续跟踪所负责细分行业的动态趋势，定期输出行业研究报告、竞争格局分析及投资策略建议，为公司投资决策提供数据与逻辑支撑。 6. 其他任务 ：完成上级交办的其他相关工作。 岗位要求： 1.教育背景：硕士及以上学历，理工科专业背景（计算机、人工智能、电气工程、自动化、数学、物理等优先）；同时具备金融、经济或管理类复合学历者优先。 2.工作经验： · 具有2年以上知名PE/VC机构、产业投资部门或3年以上头部投行相关岗位工作经验，且在半导体、AI、机器人、先进制造等领域有直接投资或深度行业研究经历； · 有上述领域研发、产品经理或技术管理经验者优先考虑（可适当放宽投资年限）。 3.专业能力： · 对所负责行业有深入理解，熟悉技术演进路径、产业链格局及商业模式演变，具备独立判断项目技术壁垒和市场前景的能力； · 具备扎实的财务建模、估值分析和风险控制能力，能独立完成项目尽调和投资报告。 4.综合素质： · 热爱科技创新，对新兴技术趋势和商业新模式保持高度敏感与好奇心； · 拥有较强的学习能力、逻辑思维和沟通协调能力，善于与创业团队、行业专家及内部团队高效协作； · 具备良好的职业操守和抗压能力，能够适应快节奏的工作环境； · 具备使用英语作为工作语言的能力。

requirements: At Lenovo, we're looking for an inspiring sales leader who is passionate about developing people, building strong customer relationships, and driving business growth. As our Sales Manager – Large Enterprise , you'll lead a team of Client Managers responsible for some of Lenovo's most strategic enterprise customers across the UK and Ireland. Drive sales performance by delivering against revenue, profitability, pipeline and customer satisfaction objectives. Support your team in identifying new business opportunities, expanding existing customer relationships and increasing share of wallet across Lenovo's portfolio. Build trusted relationships with senior customer stakeholders, including executive decision-makers, to strengthen Lenovo's strategic position. Champion operational excellence by embedding best practices across sales processes, opportunity qualification and customer engagement. Strong track record of delivering revenue growth through coaching, strategic account management and sales execution. Excellent knowledge of pipeline management, forecasting, opportunity qualification and account planning methodologies. Commercially minded with the ability to identify growth opportunities and drive cross-sell and upsell strategies. Strong leadership, coaching and people development skills with the ability to motivate diverse teams. Confidence engaging with senior business leaders, including C-suite stakeholders. Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to inform decisions, improve performance and drive business outcomes. Excellent communication, collaboration and influencing skills, with a passion for delivering outstanding customer experiences. Role Overview As MEA Desktop 4P Manager for the Commercial business, you will lead Lenovo’s desktop portfolio strategy across the Middle East and Africa. The role is responsible for defining and executing the right product, pricing, promotion, and channel plans to drive revenue growth, improve profitability, strengthen market share, and reinforce Lenovo’s competitive position across countries and territories. Key Responsibilities 4P Strategy and Business Planning: Define and execute the MEA Commercial Desktop 4P strategy across product portfolio, pricing, channel placement, and promotional planning. Revenue and Profitability Management: Manage product line-up, pricing, promotions, rebates, and sales programs to support revenue growth, margin improvement, and profitable mix expansion. Market and Competitive Analysis: Conduct regular market, customer, and competitor analysis to identify growth opportunities, sharpen product positioning, and differentiate Lenovo’s desktop offerings. Forecasting and Supply Alignment: Lead desktop demand forecasting with country and territory sales teams, ensuring alignment with sales plans, supply availability, inventory health, and aged inventory targets. Sales and Channel Execution: Partner with country and territory sales teams to support weekly sales-out priorities, stock reviews, campaign execution, channel pricing, and promotional activities. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with regional and global product teams, marketing, alliances, operations, and sales stakeholders to implement commercial desktop strategies and support business growth initiatives. Performance Management: Track performance against revenue, profitability, product mix, inventory, and market share objectives, and recommend corrective actions where required to stay on plan. Customer and Product Positioning: Support sales and customer discussions by understanding business needs, recommending suitable desktop solutions, and positioning Lenovo offerings effectively against alternatives. Position Requirements Minimum of 5 years of experience in product management, 4P, business management, commercial channel management, or a related role. Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field preferred. Strong understanding of the commercial PC business, desktop portfolio management, and channel dynamics across the Middle East and Africa. Proven ability to manage pricing, promotions, rebates, product mix, forecasting, and business performance against revenue and profitability targets. Strong analytical skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to translate data into clear business insights and actions. Good understanding of financial metrics, P&L drivers, inventory KPIs, margin management, and business planning processes. Effective communicator and influencer, able to present strategies, recommendations, and product positioning clearly to sales teams, leadership, and business partners. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. Our team is expanding its in-house design capabilities and is seeking an experienced Senior Mechanical Engineer with a strong background in server design. As part of Lenovo’s Infrastructure Solutions Group (ISG), you will contribute to the development of next-generation server products that help solve some of the world’s most complex computing challenges. In this role, you will collaborate with engineering teams across Lenovo’s global design centers to develop common building-block designs and deliver high-quality, innovative server solutions. You will be involved throughout the full product lifecycle—from concept development and design validation to market release and field sustainability. The fast-paced server industry demands solutions that are faster, smaller, and more energy efficient. As technologies continue to evolve, this role offers the opportunity to tackle complex engineering challenges while helping shape the future of Lenovo server products. Responsibilities: Design and develop next-generation server platforms, including: Printed circuit board (PCB) layouts Structural sheet metal enclosures Plastic components and assemblies Partner with system architects and cross-functional engineering teams to define and finalize product concepts. Create detailed mechanical designs and engineering documentation using industry-standard CAD tools. Develop, prototype, validate, and optimize mechanical designs to meet performance, quality, cost, and manufacturability requirements. Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve design issues throughout the product development lifecycle. Collaborate closely with thermal, power, system, manufacturing, and other engineering teams to ensure successful product delivery. Contribute to common building-block designs used across Lenovo's server portfolio. Perform engineering change management activities and maintain design documentation. Develop and manage bills of materials (BOMs), cost estimates, and tolerance analyses. Support product introduction, qualification testing, and field sustainment activities. Provide regular project updates and report weekly design status to management. Ensure designs comply with industry standards, regulatory requirements, and product quality objectives. Basic Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering Minimum of 10 years of mechanical design experience in server, storage, telecommunications, or related hardware product development. Minimum of 10 years of experience using PTC Creo for 3D mechanical design. Minimum of 10 years of experience using PTC Windchill for product data and CAD file management. Strong experience with Sheet metal design, Plastic part design, Mechanical assemblies and integration Experience managing engineering changes and product lifecycle documentation. Preferred Qualifications: Direct experience designing enterprise server platforms and rack-mounted systems. Hands-on experience assembling, debugging, and testing server hardware. Experience using basic mechanical fabrication and assembly tools. Familiarity with manufacturing processes and design for manufacturability (DFM). Knowledge of thermal and power system integration considerations in server products. Experience supporting products through launch, sustainment, and field issue resolution. requirements: Position Overview Lenovo is seeking an experienced PR & Communications Manager to lead strategic communications, media relations, executive thought leadership, and content development for our Solutions & Services business in Hong Kong. This role will be instrumental in positioning Lenovo as a leader in AI, digital transformation, and technology solutions by driving compelling storytelling, executive visibility, and customer-centric communications. The successful candidate will be a strategic communicator with excellent writing skills, strong relationships across Hong Kong's Tier 1 business and technology media, and the ability to influence senior stakeholders. Fluency in both English and Cantonese is essential. Key Responsibilities Strategic Communications Develop and execute integrated communications strategies that support Lenovo's AI and Solutions & Services business priorities. Create compelling narratives that strengthen Lenovo's reputation as a trusted technology solutions provider. Partner with regional and global communications, marketing, and business teams to ensure consistent messaging across all external communications. Media Relations Build and maintain strong relationships with Tier 1 business, technology, and trade media in Hong Kong. Develop and execute proactive media relations strategies that generate high-impact earned coverage for Lenovo's Solutions & Services business. Write, edit, and distribute press releases, media alerts, executive statements, and other press materials that communicate Lenovo's business milestones, product announcements, partnerships, customer successes, and thought leadership initiatives. Manage media inquiries, interviews, press briefings, and executive engagements. Collaborate with external PR agencies to develop compelling media narratives, story angles, and integrated communications campaigns. Monitor media coverage, identify emerging opportunities, and provide insights and recommendations to strengthen Lenovo's brand presence. Thought Leadership & Content Develop thought leadership campaigns that position Lenovo as an AI and solutions leader. Create compelling content including executive bylines, articles, customer stories, opinion pieces, and media pitches. Work with internal subject matter experts and agency partners to translate complex technology topics into engaging business-focused content. Support paid and earned amplification strategies to maximize content reach among target audiences. Executive Communications Elevate the profile of Lenovo's Hong Kong-based Solutions & Services leaders through strategic communications, media engagement, speaking opportunities, and thought leadership. Develop executive messaging, presentation materials, media briefing documents, and keynote content. Manage executive LinkedIn content strategies that build credibility and industry influence. Events & Speaking Opportunities Identify and secure speaking opportunities at leading industry conferences, business forums, and technology events. Build relationships with event organizers and industry associations to increase Lenovo's executive visibility. Support keynote presentations, panel discussions, and executive media appearances. Customer Storytelling Showcase customer success stories that demonstrate measurable business outcomes enabled by Lenovo's Solutions & Services portfolio. Collaborate with sales, marketing, and customer teams to identify compelling customer references and transform them into external communications assets. Ensure customer-centric messaging is consistently reflected across media, executive communications, and thought leadership content. Qualifications Required Experience: Bachelor's degree in Communications, Public Relations, Journalism, Marketing, or a related discipline. 7–10 years of experience in corporate communications, public relations, or executive communications. Experience in the technology, enterprise IT, consulting, or B2B sectors is highly preferred. Demonstrated success developing executive thought leadership and media engagement strategies. Language Requirements: Fluent written and spoken English. Fluent written and spoken Cantonese. Mandarin proficiency is an advantage. Skills & Competencies Established relationships with Hong Kong Tier 1 business and technology media. Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills. Strong executive presence and experience working with senior business leaders. Excellent project management and stakeholder management skills. Experience managing PR agencies and cross-functional communications programs. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, global matrix organization. Strong understanding of digital communications, LinkedIn thought leadership, and integrated media strategies. Additional

requirements: Position Description The General Procurement organization is responsible for sourcing, purchasing, and managing spend for all non-production goods or services used internally by Lenovo. Major operating expense categories: RD, marketing, and workforce. The General Procurement team is responsible for leading the following activities: - Working with business unit/budget owner (our internal Lenovo stakeholders), on appropriate spending strategy, including industry best practices, category/market trend analysis, and supplier capabilities; - Appropriately selecting Supplier sources based on driving competitive sourcing activities; - Forming contracts with selected Suppliers to help ensure both deliverables and risk avoidance; - Managing Supplier performance via stakeholder feedback and performance-based metrics; - Ensuring appropriate business controls are in place to protect Lenovo. Job Responsibilities： As a sourcing professional, this candidate will take the lead in multi-category indirect procurement management, responsible for developing and executing category management strategies, optimizing end-to-end sourcing processes for non-production goods and services across various categories. He/she should have extensive experience in indirect procurement covering multiple categories, proactively engage internal stakeholders to provide strategic sourcing solutions, drive high-impact procurement initiatives, and act as the core commercial lead for cross-category spend optimization and supplier ecosystem management. The candidate will be responsible for six (6) primary business metrics: - Manage and optimize Lenovo’s cross-category spend with external suppliers, generating significant Procurement Value-Add through strategic sourcing and category management; - Drive Customer Satisfaction (Serviceability) for internal executives and operations teams via scientific supplier selection, category strategy alignment, and proactive engagement; - Ensure robust Business Controls throughout sourcing, contracting, spending, and supplier management processes; - Develop and promote best practices, lead transformation initiatives to achieve continuous improvement in category procurement efficiency and effectiveness; - Initiate and optimize procure-to-pay processes for non-strategic purchases, enhancing overall procurement operational efficiency; - Formulate and implement category management strategies to optimize category structure, reduce supply chain risks, and improve long-term supply stability. Job Requirements： The selected candidate will exhibit the following abilities: - Analytics -- Proficient in competitive bid analysis, cost analysis, fair-value analysis, and scenario modeling, with the ability to translate data into category strategy insights; - Category Management -- Capable of developing and executing category management strategies, analyzing category lifecycle, and optimizing category procurement mix across multiple indirect categories; - Problem solving -- Ability to understand core objectives, identify key obstacles, conduct in-depth root cause analysis, mitigate cross-category supply risks, and develop feasible alternative solutions; - Negotiation -- Expertise in conducting fact-based, high-level negotiations with suppliers, driving favorable commercial terms for cross-category collaborations; - Team Collaboration -- Ability to communicate and collaborate effectively, drive cross-functional and cross-regional work successfully in a matrix team environment; - Independence -- Strong self-management ability to complete complex tasks and strategic initiatives with minimal supervision; - Fact-seeking -- Proactive in conducting in-depth research on goods/services across multiple categories to support strategy development and negotiation advantages; - Project Management -- Excellent ability to prioritize strategic and operational activities, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and maximize overall procurement value. - Indirect procurement experience covering multiple categories/ RD demand is plus; - Proven track record in category management strategy development and execution is highly desirable; - 2-3 years’ professional experience in spend management and strategic sourcing is required; - Bachelors Degree at least; - Fluent in oral and written English Additional

requirements: Position Overview Lead the architecture design, technology innovation, and development of next-generation power products, including GaN chargers, desktop charging solutions, power banks, docking power solutions, and emerging energy products. Drive power technology roadmaps, common platform development, and technical collaboration with ODM/OEM partners to deliver industry-leading solutions for global customers. Job Responsibilities Define system architecture and key technology strategies for power products ranging from 65W to 400W+, covering power conversion, fast charging protocols, power allocation, thermal management, EMC/EMI, safety, and reliability. Lead development of chargers, power banks, docking power solutions, and custom enterprise power products, ensuring delivery targets for quality, cost, performance, and schedule. Build and evolve reusable power platforms to improve technology reuse, development efficiency, and portfolio competitiveness. Drive technical engagement with ODM/OEM partners, evaluate technical solutions, manage risks, and resolve complex design and manufacturing challenges. Monitor industry trends, explore emerging technologies, and generate innovations and patents that strengthen Lenovo's competitive advantage. Job Qualifications Bachelor's degree or above in Power Electronics, Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Automation, or related fields. 10+ years of experience in power electronics product development, with proven expertise in chargers, adapters, fast charging technologies, and high-power power systems. Strong knowledge of LLC, ACF, QR Flyback, Active Clamp Flyback, Synchronous Rectification, USB PD3.1 EPR, PPS, and major charging protocols. Solid understanding of thermal design, EMC/EMI, safety compliance, reliability engineering, DFM/DFA, and mass production processes. Experience with GaN power architectures, high-power products (140W+), platform development, and global ODM/OEM collaboration is highly preferred. Demonstrated technical leadership, system-level thinking, innovation capability, and strong execution skills. Patent contributions in power electronics is must. Additional
responsibilities: Define system architecture and key technology strategies for power products ranging from 65W to 400W+, covering power conversion, fast charging protocols, power allocation, thermal management, EMC/EMI, safety, and reliability. Lead development of chargers, power banks, docking power solutions, and custom enterprise power products, ensuring delivery targets for quality, cost, performance, and schedule. Build and evolve reusable power platforms to improve technology reuse, development efficiency, and portfolio competitiveness. Drive technical engagement with ODM/OEM partners, evaluate technical solutions, manage risks, and resolve complex design and manufacturing challenges. Monitor industry trends, explore emerging technologies, and generate innovations and patents that strengthen Lenovo's competitive advantage. Job

requirements: 一、岗位职责 自主开拓：依托自身自有能源电力客户资源，独立负责机器人巡检产品（主要为轮足机器狗等）在电网、发电行业的客户对接、需求挖掘、方案输出、商务谈判及招投标全流程，实现个人销售业绩目标、回款目标，打造标杆项目案例。 团队协同：牵头协同区域销售团队，制定区域市场拓展策略，分享客户资源、行业经验及成单技巧，指导区域销售开展客户开发、项目跟进工作，协助解决区域拓展中的难点问题，推动区域整体销售目标达成。 客户深耕：维护自身核心客户关系，同时联动区域销售，搭建覆盖国网、南网（省/市/县级）、大型能源集团（国能、华能、大唐等）的全域客户体系，挖掘客户二次合作及转介绍机会，提升客户粘性与忠诚度。 市场引领：调研能源电力行业巡检领域政策导向、市场动态、竞品情况及技术趋势，结合公司产品优势，输出市场分析报告及拓展建议，参与公司市场策略、产品迭代及解决方案优化的研讨。 渠道搭建：协同区域销售拓展行业内渠道商、集成商资源，建立战略合作关系，整合内外部资源，提升产品在能源电力领域的市场覆盖度与渗透率。 二 、 岗位要求 （一）核心硬性要求 学历：本科及以上学历，市场营销、电力工程、自动化、机器人相关专业优先 行业资历： 8 年及以上能源电力行业（电网、发电）机器人巡检业务拓展/销售经验 ，具备资深业务拓展能力，熟悉行业发展规律、采购流程、预算体系及决策链条。 成单能力：具备 独立成单经验 ，有机器人巡检产品（机器狗、巡检机器人等）在能源电力场景的成功成单案例，有百万级及以上项目操盘经验者优先。 客户资源： 拥有稳定、优质的自有能源电力客户资源 ，可直接对接国网、南网、大型能源集团核心决策层，能快速启动业务、达成业绩突破。 协同能力：具备团队协同与指导能力，有带领区域销售团队、协同拓展市场的经验，能有效整合资源、赋能团队，推动区域整体业绩提升。 （二）能力素质要求 商务能力：具备卓越的商务谈判、沟通表达及资源整合能力，善于捕捉客户需求，能独立应对复杂商务场景，高效推进项目落地。 市场敏感度：能精准把握能源电力行业政策机遇与市场需求变化，具备较强的市场分析、预判及开拓能力，能主动挖掘潜在客户及合作机会。 管理协同：具备良好的团队管理、指导及协同能力，能快速联动区域销售、技术、交付等部门，高效解决项目推进及团队拓展中的各类问题。 抗压与执行：目标感极强，工作积极主动，能承受高强度业绩压力，具备优秀的时间管理能力、执行力及攻坚能力，可适应高频行业出差。 职业素养：诚信正直，具备强烈的责任心、敬业精神及行业深耕意识，认同公司产品理念，愿意与公司、团队共同成长，打造行业核心竞争力。 Additional

requirements: Sobre a posição Estamos em busca de uma pessoa dinâmica, organizada e com excelente habilidade de comunicação para atuar na interface entre times internos e parceiros externos. Essa pessoa terá um papel fundamental no acompanhamento de iniciativas relacionadas a serviços financeiros digitais, garantindo alinhamento entre áreas, acompanhamento de indicadores e suporte à execução de projetos e campanhas. Buscamos alguém com perfil colaborativo, que goste de se relacionar com diferentes stakeholders, tenha facilidade para transitar entre áreas de negócio e tecnologia e consiga acompanhar múltiplas frentes simultaneamente. Principais responsabilidades Atuar como ponto de contato entre parceiros externos e equipes internas. Apoiar o relacionamento e a comunicação com parceiros estratégicos. Coordenar e acompanhar demandas envolvendo diferentes áreas da empresa, como Vendas, Produto, Operações, Tecnologia e Negócios. Garantir o alinhamento entre stakeholders internos e externos durante a execução de projetos e iniciativas. Acompanhar cronogramas, entregas e pendências, contribuindo para o andamento das atividades. Monitorar indicadores operacionais e elaborar relatórios de acompanhamento. Apoiar a execução de campanhas e iniciativas comerciais em conjunto com os times envolvidos. Identificar oportunidades de melhoria em processos e fluxos operacionais. Contribuir para a organização e documentação de processos, atividades e projetos. Requisitos Ensino superior completo ou em andamento em Administração, Engenharia, Economia, Sistemas de Informação, Tecnologia ou áreas correlatas. Boa capacidade de comunicação verbal e escrita. Facilidade para se relacionar com diferentes públicos e áreas da empresa. Perfil organizado, proativo e com forte senso de responsabilidade. Capacidade de acompanhar múltiplas demandas simultaneamente. Boa capacidade analítica e atenção aos detalhes. Inglês avançado. Diferenciais Experiência prévia em operações, atendimento corporativo, customer success, parcerias, vendas consultivas ou gestão de projetos. Vivência em empresas de tecnologia, fintechs, bancos digitais ou meios de pagamento. Familiaridade com indicadores de negócio e elaboração de relatórios. Interesse por produtos financeiros digitais e soluções de crédito. Perfil que buscamos Pessoa comunicativa e com boa desenvoltura para interação com parceiros e diferentes áreas. Capacidade de construir relacionamentos e atuar de forma colaborativa. Perfil "hands-on", com disposição para aprender e assumir novas responsabilidades. Flexibilidade para atuar em um ambiente dinâmico e em constante evolução. Interesse em entender o negócio de forma ampla e contribuir para o sucesso das iniciativas da área #MBG Additional

About our Team The Manufacturing Test Strategy and Automation team is responsible for delivering test platforms and visualization solutions to support the manufacturing of Lenovo Servers, Storage, and Rack products. As our product portfolio evolves and grows into new strategic areas, we have an opening for a Software Engineer to develop and maintain test platform content and associated tools and utilities that enable automation and monitoring of the manufacturing test process. Our Test Platform is a framework that enables manufacturing tests to be executed in our factories and provides the means to execute tests on a variety of systems, configure the test to be run, provide run control and error handling, data and floor visualization, as well as interfacing to shop floor control and warranty databases. New features are constantly required as product architecture and content changes, as well as to provide process optimizations. The Test Platform team also develops tools for process visualization and for extracting and presenting process and quality data as required by our internal teams and customers. Lenovo has manufacturing facilities in The United States, China, Hungary, Mexico and Brazil and the expectation is that travel to these sites would be required as part of this role. Responsibilities: Platform Test Engineering is responsible for designing, implementing, testing, and maintaining processes and code for our test platform and visualization tools. You will be working with Local and global teams to identify, quantify and solve problems and improve overall procedures through automation and internal tool development. Interface with multiple fulfillment sites to enable and manage solution delivery without impacting production. Design, develop, and maintain code to provide platform and tool solutions Using Python 3 for code development Vue for web frameworks (JavaScript) MSQL for database queries Use data from IT systems and shop floor control to develop real time visualization tools On-Site support and review of ongoing challenges Data Driven Engineering – create tools and processes to support manufacturing and testing based on actual site performance Interface with multiple fulfillment sites to enable and manage solution delivery without impacting production. Basic Requirements: 3-5+ years of programming experience Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Computer Engineering, or a related engineering degree program Proficient in Python3.x Vue for web frameworks MSQL for database queries Preferred Requirements: You will be successful in this position if you are detail oriented, methodical, and organized, and like to work through complex problems A master’s degree in computer science, Computer Engineering, or a similar field would be helpful Experience with developing manufacturing tools, working with manufacturing systems, and test processes Experience with complex process development and deployment Self-motivated team player Able to apply engineering concepts Effective communicator Additional

experience: 10–15+ years in Digital Workplace, end user computing, or managed services
requirements: As a Principal Digital Transformation Consultant (FDE Track), you will lead strategic workplace modernization for enterprise customers while also acting as a forward‑deployed technical leader during early deployment and MVP phases. You combine executive‑level advisory, solution architecture, and hands‑on technical orchestration to accelerate time‑to‑value across Lenovo DWS, DaaS, and Lenovo xIQ. You will embed with lighthouse customers to run discovery, shape roadmaps, configure early experiences, prototype automation or telemetry patterns, and ensure customer value is realized rapidly. You will bridge business strategy, product thinking, and pragmatic engineering execution, guiding programs from blueprint → MVP → pilot → scale → steady state. Key Responsibilities: 1) Strategy, Value Advisory & Executive Leadership - Lead C‑suite and VP‑level strategy workshops to align vision, value drivers, AI adoption priorities, and operating model shifts. - Build board‑ready business cases and P&L models quantifying TCO, OpEx/CapEx mix, cost‑to‑serve improvements, productivity lift, and experience impact. - Translate AI/automation, UEM, and collaboration programs into investment theses and risk narratives for executives. 2) Forward‑Deployed Engagement & Early Value Realization (FDE) - Embed with customers during early engagement to drive hands‑on discovery, process validation, and rapid prototyping. - Build or orchestrate lightweight integrations, automations, telemetry mappings, and xIQ insights needed to achieve the first measurable outcomes. - Configure early deployment patterns across provisioning, xIQ dashboards, automation rules, or experience telemetry readiness. - Own the first working version of the solution in partnership with SAs, delivery, and product. - Ensure early value milestones (e.g., MTTR reduction, automation coverage, Autopilot/UEM readiness) are achieved quickly. 3) GTM Leadership & Customer Journey Excellence - Drive end‑to‑end customer journeys from assessment → blueprint → MVP → scale → steady state. - Partner with Sales, Offering, and Marketing to craft value narratives and pursuit strategies. - Mentor pursuit teams and raise win‑rates through improved storytelling, qualification, and solution strength. 4) AI‑Enabled Operating Model & HITL Governance - Create hybrid AI + human operating models with clearly defined HITL touchpoints, safety constraints, and governance. - Define escalation paths, decision boundaries, and quality guardrails for predictive and agentic automation patterns. - Prioritize AI and automation use cases, guiding rollout into proactive support and self‑heal scenarios using Lenovo xIQ. 5) Deep Process Discovery & FDE‑Driven Product Goal Creation - Run hands‑on discovery using workshops, telemetry, analytics, and frontline shadowing. - Translate discovery insights into product goals, user stories, and measurable outcomes. - Shape MVPs, automation packs, and roadmap increments with DWS, xIQ, and Offering teams. - Validate hypotheses through customer‑embedded prototypes and pilots. 6) Solution Architecture & Hands‑On Orchestration - Convert business outcomes into end‑to‑end architecture across DWS, DaaS, UEM/Autopilot, collaboration, security, and modern support. - Act as forward‑deployed orchestrator during MVP: configure telemetry, align identity/security, tune automation rules, validate xIQ dashboards. - Partner with architects and delivery to ensure feasibility, scalability, and optimization. 7) Program Governance, Change Leadership & Value Tracking - Establish program governance with milestones, KPIs, risks, and dependencies. - Lead stakeholder communications, executive updates, and operational readiness. - Track value realization throughout MVP → pilot → scale. 8) Product Management Mindset & Reusable IP Creation - Operate with product‑owner discipline for AI/ML platforms and automation at scale. - Manage backlog, MVP scope, pilot feedback loops, and release cadence. - Convert customer patterns into reusable IP—frameworks, automation packs, telemetry templates. Required Qualifications: • Experience: 10–15+ years in Digital Workplace, end user computing, or managed services; 5+ years leading complex, multi tower transformation programs with executive visibility. Preferred Qualifications: • AI & Automation: Practical knowledge of Predictive AI, Generative AI, and Agentic patterns; proven ability to frame use cases, risks, and controls for executive decision making. • Financial Acumen: Strong command of business case development, P&L impact modeling, TCO/ROI, and cloud economics; ability to map IT investment to business value. • Workshops & Co Creation: Exceptional facilitation skills (discovery, value, design, governance) with a record of co creating roadmaps and gaining stakeholder alignment. • Solution Depth: Hands on familiarity with modern deployment, UEM, collaboration, identity/security, DEX/experience analytics (Lenovo xIQ or equivalent), and service desk modernization. • Product Mindset: Experience owning the lifecycle of a complex, data intensive enterprise product (AI/ML platform or large scale automation preferred). • Industry Exposure: Direct experience in healthcare, retail, and/or telco, with ability to adapt patterns to domain outcomes. • Communication: Outstanding executive storytelling, negotiation, and written/verbal communication across business and technical audiences. • Leadership: Mentors senior consultants/architects; influences cross functional teams; collaborates across Sales, Offering, Delivery, and Partners. We follow a friendly hybrid model with three days a week in the office—great for collaboration and connection! We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: Procurement Strategy Project Manager This role is ideal for professionals who thrive in highly complex, cross-functional environments and enjoy leading strategic initiatives across engineering, product development, supply chain, and procurement functions. You will serve as a key program leader, influencing product decisions from concept through mass production while ensuring cost competitiveness, supply readiness, and business success. What You'll Do Lead Strategic Cross-Functional Programs Act as the Procurement Program Lead within new product development teams, partnering closely with Engineering, Product Management, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Sales, Marketing, and Procurement stakeholders. Drive alignment across diverse teams to ensure products are delivered on schedule, meet business objectives, and achieve operational readiness. Lead discussions, facilitate decision-making, and influence stakeholders without direct authority in a highly matrixed global environment. Own Product Commercial & Supply Strategy Shape sourcing and supply strategies throughout the product lifecycle, from concept development to production ramp. Provide strategic guidance on: Product cost structure Supplier ecosystem strategy Multi-sourcing approaches Supply continuity planning Commercial risk mitigation Influence product design and sourcing decisions that improve competitiveness, flexibility, and long-term scalability. Drive New Product Introduction (NPI) Partner with product and engineering teams during the entire NPI lifecycle. Analyze the impact of design changes, market conditions, and supply constraints on program execution. Ensure procurement and supply chain readiness while supporting rapid product evolution in fast-moving AI and data center environments. Manage Complex Stakeholder Ecosystems Coordinate priorities across multiple teams with competing objectives. Resolve conflicts, drive trade-off decisions, and build consensus among technical and business stakeholders. Act as a trusted advisor who brings together diverse perspectives to achieve the best outcome for the program. Anticipate Risk & Enable Execution Excellence Identify potential supply, supplier, commercial, and execution risks early in the development cycle. Work cross-functionally to develop mitigation plans that protect project milestones and launch commitments. Maintain agility and execution discipline in a rapidly changing technology landscape. What Makes You Successful We're looking for someone who combines strong program management capability with strategic business thinking. Core Competencies Cross-functional Program Management Stakeholder Management & Influence Executive Communication Conflict Resolution & Negotiation Preferred Experience You may come from backgrounds such as: Technical Program Management Product Development Program Management Engineering Program Management NPI Program Management Supply Chain Program Management Strategic Sourcing Program Management Procurement Program Management Additional Requirements Comfortable influencing stakeholders without formal authority. Experience leading initiatives involving multiple global teams. Strong English communication skills with the confidence to lead discussions in international settings. Comfortable working across global time zones and collaborating with teams worldwide. Able to thrive in fast-paced environments where priorities and requirements continuously evolve. Why This Role is Different This is not a traditional buyer or commodity procurement role. You will function as a strategic program leader, helping shape next-generation AI platforms by connecting technology, business strategy, supplier ecosystems, and global execution teams. You'll work at the intersection of innovation, product development, and commercial strategy—driving decisions that directly impact Lenovo's AI and data center growth journey. Additional

requirements: We are looking for Services and Solutions Training Manager for AP region. Learning and upskilling are a key area of focus for SSG and Lenovo. As we are moving to a more services and solution driven business, sellers will need to change their approach to a consultative sales methodology. The offers are much more customizable, so sellers need to learn about the different solution building blocks and how those can deliver business outcomes to our customers. Sellers need to address new personas in their account, learn to customize their messaging and must work with more stakeholders internally, requiring usage of new process and tools. We also need to train our partner account sales reps to sell to partners and train sellers of these partners. What You will Do: Develop and implement quarterly AP training plans by sales persona, aligned with organizational needs and global strategy. Act as the single point of contact (SPOC) for AP sales management, working closely with local enablement, global training teams, and sales leaders. Set learning objectives, select methodologies, manage content and trainers, and oversee logistics, budget, and KPIs for learning initiatives. Ensure sellers and partners are equipped to sell services and solutions through consultative approaches, addressing new personas and using updated tools. Measure training effectiveness, track consumption, and continuously improve programs based on feedback and performance data. Train partner account managers and partner sellers to effectively position and sell Lenovo’s service offerings. Participate in shaping the global training strategy and innovate with new approaches, including AI-supported learning experiences. Position Requirements: Ability to design and deliver sales-focused training programs tailored to business needs and continuously improve learning experiences. Skilled in various techniques including e-learning, workshops, role plays, and instructional design. Strong understanding of how adults learn to ensure training is engaging and effective. Experience in the IT sector, with familiarity in high-tech sales or services environments. Excellent presentation skills and the ability to lead, motivate, and support trainers and trainees. Capable of managing multiple learning programs, schedules, and operational tasks efficiently. Comfortable working in fast-paced environments with flexibility, dependability, and a creative mindset. BA/BS degree with 3+ years in training, curriculum design, and working with management and decision-makers. 3+ years using different training methodologies, incl ownership for F2F trainings (incl AI is a plus.) requirements: Senior Associate, Legal – Privacy (Marketing & E-Commerce) In this role, you will join a dynamic Legal and Privacy team focused on shaping how customer data is used responsibly across digital marketing and e-commerce channels. You will play a key part in ensuring that innovative marketing and advertising activities are built on strong, compliant, and privacy-conscious foundations. This is a highly impactful position where legal expertise meets digital innovation. You will work closely with marketing, e-commerce, and technical teams to enable data-driven growth while ensuring compliance with global privacy regulations. Your work will directly influence how customer trust, transparency, and privacy are maintained across online experiences. We are looking for someone who enjoys working at the intersection of law, technology, and digital business—someone who can translate complex privacy requirements into practical, business-friendly solutions. What You'll Do Advise marketing and e-commerce teams on privacy matters related to digital advertising, customer data usage, cross-device tracking, retargeting, email marketing, and social media campaigns. Review, draft, and negotiate privacy and data protection agreements with ad tech partners, analytics providers, marketing platforms, and data collaboration/cleanroom vendors. Develop and maintain privacy documentation including cookie banners, consent flows, privacy notices, and opt-out mechanisms. Ensure compliance with global privacy frameworks such as GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, ePrivacy Directive, and emerging digital regulation. Support the implementation and optimisation of Consent Management Platforms (CMPs) and user preference management tools across digital properties. Partner with regional legal teams, data governance, and engineering stakeholders to embed privacy-by-design principles into marketing and e-commerce initiatives. Monitor regulatory developments, enforcement trends, and industry standards impacting digital marketing and advertising technologies. Deliver privacy training and create clear, practical guidance and best practices for marketing, product, and digital teams. Act as a trusted advisor, helping teams balance innovation, customer experience, and privacy compliance. What We're Looking For Qualified lawyer (JD or equivalent) with active licence to practise law in relevant jurisdictions. Minimum 5 years of legal experience with a strong focus on privacy and data protection. Proven experience working with digital marketing, ad tech, e-commerce, or technology-driven businesses (in-house or law firm experience welcome). Strong understanding of global privacy laws and how they apply to online behavioural advertising and customer data ecosystems. Hands-on experience with ad tech environments, including cookies, pixels, DMPs, CDPs, identity resolution, and real-time bidding ecosystems. Familiarity with privacy technologies such as Consent Management Platforms (CMPs) and user preference management tools. Ability to translate complex legal and regulatory requirements into clear, actionable guidance for non-legal stakeholders. Strong communication, stakeholder management, and influencing skills across technical and business teams. Privacy certifications such as CIPP/E, CIPP/US, CIPM (or equivalent) are a strong advantage. What We Offer You Life Insurance Private Medical Insurance Pension Plan Multisport Card Employee Assistance Program (health, legal & financial consultancy support) Access to Internal E-Learning Development Platform We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any federal, state, or local protected class. requirements: ***Please Note: This is a fully remote role; however, international travel is expected to collaborate with global R&D labs, business units, and pilot customers.*** About Lenovo CTO Org Lenovo CTO Org is the cutting-edge R&D and technology incubation organization of Lenovo, dedicated to advancing information technology, smart devices, and services. Our mission is to transform breakthrough innovations into scalable businesses, powering Lenovo’s most advanced products and shaping the future of AI, edge computing, intelligent devices, and enterprise solutions. About the Role This is a hands-on technical role within the AI Solution Delivery organization of Lenovo CTO Org’s Tech Incubation team, reporting to the Director, AI Solution Delivery. You will be responsible for deploying and operationalizing AI-powered solutions in real customer environments. Working closely with Product Management, Solution Architects, R&D teams, and customers, you will bridge the gap between prototype and production by implementing, integrating, tuning, and validating solutions on-site. Success in this role requires a combination of software engineering, applied AI, systems integration, and customer-facing problem-solving. You must be comfortable working directly with customers, troubleshooting complex technical issues, and adapting solutions to real-world operating conditions. This role requires extensive travel and extended customer-site engagements, which may reach 70–80% depending on project needs and incubation phase. Key Responsibilities: AI Solution Deployment & Integration Deploy AI-powered solutions in customer environments, including edge computing platforms, software applications, sensors, and supporting infrastructure. Integrate solutions with customer IT and operational systems, APIs, databases, and enterprise platforms. Develop and maintain integration code, automation scripts, and deployment tooling using Python and related technologies. Assess customer environments and ensure deployment readiness, connectivity, and system compatibility. Applied AI & Solution Optimization Configure, tune, validate, and troubleshoot Computer Vision and AI models in production environments. Analyze field performance, identify root causes of accuracy issues, and optimize solution performance for real-world operating conditions. Collect and analyze deployment data to support continuous improvement and model refinement. Partner with R&D teams to communicate field findings and accelerate product maturity. Customer Delivery & Technical Leadership Serve as the primary technical lead for customer deployment activities, driving implementation execution and coordinating with architects, product managers, R&D teams, and customer stakeholders. Lead complex technical issue resolution and coordinate cross-functional engineering teams when escalations are required. Conduct customer acceptance testing and validate deployment success criteria. Train customer teams on solution operation, best practices, and troubleshooting procedures. Field Documentation & Knowledge Management Create and maintain installation guides, deployment documentation, configuration records, and troubleshooting playbooks. Document lessons learned and best practices to improve future deployments and accelerate solution scalability. Feedback Loop & Product Hardening Serve as the critical feedback channel to the core R&D team. Translate on-site deployment issues, hardware limitations, integration challenges, and algorithmic edge cases into structured, actionable engineering insights. Capture and distill delivery experience into optimization recommendations for both software and hardware components, relaying timely feedback to Solution Architects and the R&D team. Compliance, Data Privacy & IP Protection Adhere to customer safety, security, privacy, and compliance requirements during all deployment activities. Protect customer data and Lenovo intellectual property in accordance with company policies, contractual obligations, and applicable regulations. What We Look For Hands-on problem solver who enjoys working at the intersection of software, AI, systems integration, and customer deployment. Strong customer orientation and ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfortable operating in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where solutions are evolving rapidly. Highly self-directed with strong ownership and accountability. Passion for transforming innovative technologies into real-world customer success. Required Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in solution deployment, systems integration, software engineering, field implementation, or related technical roles. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, AI/ML, or a similar degree. Practical experience deploying, tuning, validating, and troubleshooting Computer Vision and AI-based solutions in production environments. Demonstrated ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues across software, infrastructure, and deployed systems. Preferred Qualifications: Strong communication and presentation skills with customer-facing experience. Experience in manufacturing, industrial automation, computer vision, AI platforms, or intelligent edge solutions is preferred. Willingness to travel extensively and support long-term customer-site deployments globally. Mandarin language proficiency is a plus. The base salary range budgeted for this position is $180k - $235k. Individuals may also be considered for bonus and/or commission. Lenovo’s various
